recording_notes: Phone filming RCA monitor — same lab as V11-V14/V17. Task start at ~3.88s absolute (first instrument visible, ~frame 115); task end at ~194.88s absolute (~frame 5846). Task time ~191.0s.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 0.0s: first instrument tip enters field, timing starts.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled entry; initial setup not immediate, costs early time.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 4.8s: needle aligned over Penrose, first bite being organized.
~task 15.3s: suture traversed drain, drawn through both marked sides.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Close to marks; exact deviation hard to verify.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 20.8s: loops formed over slit for first knot.
~task 29.3s: first throw seated, slit approximated without avulsion.
~task 47.3s: second throw forming after regrasping/post management.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Likely single alternating throw; extra regrasping.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 65.3s: third throw cinched with knot stack centered.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternating single throw, moderate confidence on lead.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 90.3s: additional security manipulation continues after required three.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extra throws or repeated tightening prolong task.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 122.3s: knot manipulation continues, drain still approximated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Stable control; economy of motion is the scoring limiter.

frame_analyses.description: ~task 181.3s: cutter enters and lines up with tails.
~task 191.3s: both suture ends cut, task complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Closure intact, no visible avulsion or gap.

knot_assessments.notes: Double-wrap surgeon's knot; left lead (moderate confidence).
Single alternating throw; right lead (moderate confidence) after regrasping.
Single alternating throw; extra security throws follow, exact endpoint approximate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible residual gap after tightening or after tails cut.

technique_summary: Task completed within time limit without drain avulsion; final knot centered with no visible gap after cutting. Main score limiter was efficiency — substantial time lost to regrasping, repeated tightening, and apparent extra security throws after the first three. Needle control and tissue handling generally safe.
improvement_suggestions: After seating the surgeon's knot, transition directly into two alternating single throws — stop adding extra security throws.
Load and orient the needle more decisively before the first bite.
Use smaller working loops and bring the cutter in promptly once three secure throws are complete.
strengths: No drain avulsion or gross tissue trauma.
First throw is a proper surgeon's knot.
Final closure stays approximated after cutting.
Instrument handling controlled throughout with knot stack centered.
comparison_notes: Strong agreement with Claude V19 (ΔFLS 4.0). Both teachers converge on 191-193s total time and agree on drain closure quality (no gap, no avulsion). GPT-4o assigns almost all the excess time to a single 119.7s third_throw phase (extra security throws + retightening), while Claude distributes across placement (43s) and cut (65s). Different phase boundaries, same root cause — fatigue-driven inefficiency late in the session.
